content: Piper: What’s the difference between a Brotherhood knight and a raider with a strap-on? The knight still thinks he’s the good guy while he’s fucking you raw. Deacon: Fuck, Piper, save some edge for the rest of us. But hey, at least raiders don’t pretend their power armor’s not compensating for something. Piper: Speaking of fucking fascists in tin cans, you ever catch any of those Paladins getting their rocks off at the airport? That patrol of theirs looks more like a glory hole lineup some nights. Deacon: Oh, I’ve seen ‘em. Nothing like watching a guy preach purity while his PA seals him into his own sweat and pre-cum. Bet they’d lose their shit if they knew how many synths they’ve blown their loads into. Piper: Synth rights aside, I’d rather take a Behemoth’s dick than listen to another Maxson speech. At least the mutant doesn’t pretend he’s not a monster. Deacon: Yeah, but have you tried taking a Behemoth’s dick? Asking for a friend.

Piper: Hah! If you’re asking for a friend, I’ve got a whole section in next month’s  about mutant-human relations. Spoiler: lube is mandatory. Deacon: Shit, remind me to stock up before we hit the next super mutant nest. God knows I don’t need you writing about my ass getting split open mid-op. Piper: Oh please. Like I haven’t already got enough blackmail footage from Railroad safehouse orgies. Deacon: Fuck. I knew that poncho was a bad idea.
